back_translation: Before the Passover festival, Jesus knew that His hour had come for Him to depart from this world to the Father. He had loved His own people who were in the world, and He loved them to the very end.
While the evening meal was in progress, the devil had already dropped into the heart of Judas son of Simon Iscariot the idea to betray Him.
Jesus knew that the Father had handed all things into His hand, and knew that He had come from God and was about to return to God.
He rose from the supper, removed His outer garments, and took a towel and tied it around His waist.
Then He poured water into a washing basin and began to wash the disciples' feet, and dried them with the towel He was wearing.
When He came to Simon Peter, Peter said to Him, “My Lord, are You going to wash my feet?”
Jesus answered him, “What I am doing, you do not understand at this moment, but later you will understand.”
Peter said to Him, “You will not wash my feet, ever.” Jesus answered him, “If I do not wash you, you have no part with Me.”
Simon Peter said to Him, “My Lord, not only my feet, but my hands and my head as well.”
Jesus said to him, “The one who has bathed has no need to wash anything, except only the feet, and that one is clean all over. You also are clean, but not all of you.”
For He knew who would be the one to betray Him; that is why He said, ‘Not all of you are clean.’
When He had washed their feet and put on His outer garments again, He sat down and said to them, “Do you understand what I have done for you?
You call Me ‘Teacher’ and ‘Lord,’ and you call Me rightly, for I am truly so.
If I, who am the Lord and the Teacher, have washed your feet, then you also ought to wash one another's feet.
I have set an example for you, so that you may do as I have done for you.
Amen, amen, I say to you, a slave is not greater than his master, and the one who is sent is not greater than the one who sent him.
If you know these things and do them, you will be blessed.
I am not speaking about all of you. I know those whom I have chosen. But it is so that the Scripture may be fulfilled, ‘The one who eats My bread has lifted up his heel against Me.’
I am telling you now, before the event happens, so that when it happens, you will believe that I am.
Amen, amen, I say to you, whoever receives the one I send receives Me, and whoever receives Me receives the One who sent Me.”
When Jesus had said these things, His soul was troubled, and He testified, “Amen, amen, I say to you, one of you will betray Me.”
The disciples looked at one another, not knowing of whom He was speaking.
One of His disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was reclining near to Jesus.
Simon Peter therefore made a sign to that disciple, to ask of whom He was speaking.
That disciple leaned back and rested against the breast of Jesus and said to Him, “My Lord, who is it?”
Jesus answered, “It is the one to whom I will dip this piece of bread and hand it.” Then He dipped the piece of bread and handed it to Judas son of Simon Iscariot.
After Judas received that piece of bread, Satan entered and possessed him. Jesus then said to him, “What you are about to do, please do quickly.”
But none of those at the table understood why He said that to him.
Some thought, because Judas held the money bag, that Jesus was telling him, ‘Buy what we need for the festival,’ or perhaps to give something to the poor.
When Judas had received the piece of bread, he went out at once. At that time it was night.
When Judas had gone out, Jesus said, “Now the Son of Man has received glory, and God has received glory in Him.
If God has received glory in Him, God will also cause Him to receive glory in His own self, and will do so soon.
Children, I will be with you only a little while longer. You will seek Me, and just as I told the Jewish leaders, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come,’ now I tell you so as well.
I give you a new commandment, that you love one another. Just as I have loved you, so you also must love one another.
By this everyone will know that you are My disciples — if you have love for one another.”
Simon Peter said to Him, “My Lord, where are You going?” Jesus answered, “Where I am going, you cannot follow Me at this moment, but you will follow later.”
Peter said to Him, “My Lord, why can I not follow You at this moment? I will lay down my life for You.”
Jesus answered, “Will you lay down your life for Me? Amen, amen, I say to you, before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times.”
